revision.text: #redirect[[en:Totsuyamisakitara_Kami]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''遠津山岬多良斯神'''（とおつやまさきたらしのかみ）は[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神]]。
== 概要 == : [[古事記]]にのみ登場する神で、十七世神（とおまりななよのかみ）の最後の神。初代[[八島士奴美神]]の14世孫にあたる。 : 名称は他に遠津山岬帯神とも書き、遠津は母神である遠津待根神から、帯（たらし）は[[息長帯比売命]]などの帯と同義。
== 系譜 == : [[大国主神]]の8世孫にあたる[[天日腹大科度美神]]が、[[大山津見神]]の子[[天狭霧神]]の娘[[遠津待根神]]を娶って生んだ神。十七世神の最後であり、古事記にこれ以降の系譜の記述はない。 : ただし神門臣家の系譜には遠津山崎帯の後に、[[出雲振根|振根]]の名が見える。
== 祀る神社 == * [[鳴海杻神社]] [[犬山市]]羽黒 * [[神足神社]] 現在の祭神は天神足命だが、[[本居宣長]]は本来の祭神を遠山岬多良斯津神とする。
== 関連項目 == * [[天日腹大科度美神]] * [[遠津待根神]]
[[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]]

== 系譜 == : [[大国主神]]の8世孫にあたる[[天日腹大科度美神]]が、[[大山津見神]]の子[[天狭霧神]]の娘[[遠津待根神]]を娶って生んだ神。十七世神の最後であり、古事記にこれ以降の系譜の記述はない。 : ただし神門臣家の系譜には遠津山崎帯の後に、[[出雲振根|振根]]の名が見える。
== 祀る神社 == * [[鳴海杻神社]] （[[愛知県]][[犬山市]]羽黒） * [[神足神社]] - 現在の祭神は天神足命だが、[[本居宣長]]は本来の祭神を遠山岬多良斯津神とする。

== 概要 == : [[古事記]]にのみ登場する神で、[[十七世神]]（とおまりななよのかみ）の最後の神。初代[[八島士奴美神]]の14世孫にあたる。 : 名称は他に遠津山岬帯神とも書き、遠津は母神である遠津待根神から、帯（たらし）は[[息長帯比売命]]などの帯と同義。

'''遠津山岬多良斯神'''（とおつやまさきたらしのかみ）は[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神]]。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』にのみ登場する神で、[[十七世神]]（とおまりななよのかみ）の最後の神。初代[[八島士奴美神]]の14世孫にあたる。名称は他に'''遠津山岬帯神'''とも表記される。
「遠津」は「遠方の」、「山岬」は「山の崎」、「多良斯」は「足らし」で、名義は「遠方の山の崎が満ち足りていること」と考えられる。まず「遠津」は母方の遠津待根神から承けたもので、「山岬」は山の先端を指し、そこは台地であり、山からの水が台地を潤すため穀倉地帯を形成する。「多良斯」は充足を意味し、これによって上記のような名義と解される<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
== 系譜 == [[画像:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|大国主の系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神、黄は性別不詳]] [[大国主神]]の8世孫にあたる[[天日腹大科度美神]]が、[[大山津見神]]の子[[天狭霧神]]の娘[[遠津待根神]]を娶って生んだ神。十七世神の最後であり、古事記にこれ以降の系譜の記述はない。
== 祀る神社 == * [[鳴海杻神社]] （[[愛知県]][[犬山市]]羽黒） * [[日原神社]] 境内社・山岬神社（[[島根県]][[雲南市]][[大東町]]） * [[神足神社]] - 現在の祭神は天神足命だが、[[本居宣長]]は本来の祭神を遠山岬多良斯津神とする。
== 関連項目 == * [[天日腹大科度美神]] * [[遠津待根神]]
{{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:とおつやまさきたらしのかみ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]]

== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』にのみ登場する神で、[[十七世神]]（とおまりななよのかみ）の最後の神。初代[[八島士奴美神]]の14世孫にあたる。名称は他に'''遠津山岬帯神'''とも表記される。
「遠津」は「遠方の」、「山岬」は「山の崎」、「多良斯」は「足らし」で、名義は「遠方の山の崎が満ち足りていること」と考えられる。まず「遠津」は母方の遠津待根神から承けたもので、「山岬」は山の先端を指し、そこは台地であり、山からの水が台地を潤すため穀倉地帯を形成する。「多良斯」は充足を意味し、これによって上記のような名義と解される<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
== 系譜 == [[画像:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|大国主の系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神、黄は性別不詳]] [[大国主神]]の8世孫にあたる[[天日腹大科度美神]]が、[[大山津見神]]の子[[天狭霧神]]の娘[[遠津待根神]]を娶って生んだ神。十七世神の最後であり、古事記にこれ以降の系譜の記述はない。
== 祀る神社 == * [[鳴海杻神社]] （[[愛知県]][[犬山市]]羽黒） * [[日原神社]] 境内社・山岬神社（[[島根県]][[雲南市]][[大東町]]） * [[神足神社]] - 現在の祭神は天神足命だが、[[本居宣長]]は本来の祭神を遠山岬多良斯津神とする。
== 参考文献 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[天日腹大科度美神]] * [[遠津待根神]]
{{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:とおつやまさきたらしのかみ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = }} {{ill|{{PAGENAME}}|ja}} is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are..."
revision.text:
{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = }} {{ill|{{PAGENAME}}|ja}} is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}.
== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|en}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{Interlanguage link|Konohanachiruhime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto"> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.

This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shrine)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shrine)}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yaso Gods|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka Kami}} and had {{ill|Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonumamashinomaouhime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonumamashinomaouhime}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}}.
== Family Tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| {{ill|Category:17 spirits of the world|ja|Category:十七世神|fr|Category:17 esprits du monde}} [[Category:Kunitsukami]] }}

revision.comment: 外部リンクなどを追加
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 遠津山岬多良斯神 | 世代名 = [[十七世神]] 第十五代 | 先代 = [[アメノヒバラオオシナド|天日腹大科度美神]] | 次代 = | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 遠津山岬多良斯神（トオツヤマサキタラシノカミ） | 別名 = 遠津山岬帯神 | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの -->農業神 | 陵所 = | 父 = 天日腹大科度美神 | 母 = [[トオツマチネ|遠津待根神]] | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = 記載なし | 宮 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * 鳴海杻神社 * 山岬神社 等 }} | 記紀等 = 古事記 }} '''遠津山岬多良斯神'''（遠津山岬多良斯神）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]。 == 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』にのみ登場する神で、[[十七世神]]（とおまりななよのかみ）の最後の神。初代[[八島士奴美神]]の14世孫にあたる。名称は他に'''遠津山岬帯神'''とも表記される。
「遠津」は「遠方の」、「山岬」は「山の崎」、「多良斯」は「足らし」で、名義は「遠方の山の崎が満ち足りていること」と考えられる。まず「遠津」は母方の遠津待根神から承けたもので、「山岬」は山の先端を指し、そこは台地であり、山からの水が台地を潤すため穀倉地帯を形成する。「多良斯」は充足を意味し、これによって上記のような名義と解される<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。 == 系譜 == [[画像:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|大国主の系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神、黄は性別不詳]] [[大国主神]]の8世孫にあたる天日腹大科度美神が、[[大山津見神]]の子の[[天之狭霧神]]の娘の遠津待根神を娶って生んだ神。十七世神の最後であり、古事記にこれ以降の系譜の記述はない。 == 祀る神社 == * 鳴海杻神社 （[[愛知県]][[犬山市]]羽黒成海郷） - 主祭神 * 山岬神社（[[島根県]][[雲南市]][[大東町 (島根県)|大東町]]中湯石） - 祭神 : 日原神社の境内社。 * [[神足神社]]（京都府長岡京市東神足） : 現在の祭神は天神足命だが、[[本居宣長]]は本来の祭神を遠山岬多良斯津神とする。 == 参考文献 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[アメノヒバラオオシナド|天日腹大科度美神]] * [[トオツマチネ|遠津待根神]] == 外部リンク == * [https://inuyama-tabi.com/point/detail/223/ 鳴海杻神社｜犬山文化遺産ナビ] * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/unnan/4af55d34f0d2979455af525d5f9769b71dee03dc.html 日原神社 - 神社詳細 | 島根県神社庁] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とおつやまさきたらしのかみ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:十七世神]]

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 遠津山岬多良斯神 | 世代名 = [[十七世神]] 第十五代 | 先代 = [[アメノヒバラオオシナド|天日腹大科度美神]] | 次代 = | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 遠津山岬多良斯神（トオツヤマサキタラシノカミ） | 別名 = 遠津山岬帯神 | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの -->農業神 | 陵所 = | 父 = 天日腹大科度美神 | 母 = [[トオツマチネ|遠津待根神]] | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = 記載なし | 宮 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * 鳴海杻神社 * 山岬神社 等 }} | 記紀等 = [[古事記]] }} '''遠津山岬多良斯神'''（遠津山岬多良斯神）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]。 == 概要 == 『古事記』にのみ登場する神で、[[十七世神]]（とおまりななよのかみ）の最後の神。初代[[八島士奴美神]]の14世孫にあたる。名称は他に'''遠津山岬帯神'''とも表記される。
「遠津」は「遠方の」、「山岬」は「山の崎」、「多良斯」は「足らし」で、名義は「遠方の山の崎が満ち足りていること」と考えられる。まず「遠津」は母方の遠津待根神から承けたもので、「山岬」は山の先端を指し、そこは台地であり、山からの水が台地を潤すため穀倉地帯を形成する。「多良斯」は充足を意味し、これによって上記のような名義と解される<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。 == 系譜 == [[画像:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|大国主の系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神、黄は性別不詳]] [[大国主神]]の8世孫にあたる天日腹大科度美神が、[[大山津見神]]の子の[[天之狭霧神]]の娘の遠津待根神を娶って生んだ神。十七世神の最後であり、古事記にこれ以降の系譜の記述はない。 == 祀る神社 == * 鳴海杻神社 （[[愛知県]][[犬山市]]羽黒成海郷） - 主祭神 * 山岬神社（[[島根県]][[雲南市]][[大東町 (島根県)|大東町]]中湯石） - 祭神 ** 日原神社の境内社。 * [[神足神社]]（京都府長岡京市東神足） ** 現在の祭神は天神足命だが、[[本居宣長]]は本来の祭神を遠山岬多良斯津神とする。

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 遠津山岬多良斯神 | 世代名 = [[十七世神]] 第十五代 | 先代 = [[天日腹大科度美神]] | 次代 = | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 遠津山岬多良斯神（トオツヤマサキタラシノカミ） | 別名 = 遠津山岬帯神 | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの -->農業神 | 陵所 = | 父 = 天日腹大科度美神 | 母 = [[トオツマチネ|遠津待根神]] | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = 記載なし | 宮 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * 鳴海杻神社 * 山岬神社 等 }} | 記紀等 = [[古事記]] }} '''遠津山岬多良斯神'''（トオツヤマサキタラシ、{{smaller|[[歴史的仮名遣]]：}}トホツヤマサキタラシ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]。 == 概要 == 『古事記』にのみ登場する神で、[[十七世神]]（とおまりななよのかみ）の最後の神。初代[[八島士奴美神]]の14世孫にあたる。名称は他に'''遠津山岬帯神'''とも表記される。
「遠津」は「遠方の」、「山岬」は「山の崎」、「多良斯」は「足らし」で、名義は「遠方の山の崎が満ち足りていること」と考えられる。まず「遠津」は母方の遠津待根神から承けたもので、「山岬」は山の先端を指し、そこは台地であり、山からの水が台地を潤すため穀倉地帯を形成する。「多良斯」は充足を意味し、これによって上記のような名義と解される<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。 == 系譜 == [[画像:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|大国主の系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神、黄は性別不詳]] [[大国主神]]の8世孫にあたる天日腹大科度美神が、[[大山津見神]]の子の[[天之狭霧神]]の娘の遠津待根神を娶って生んだ神。十七世神の最後であり、古事記にこれ以降の系譜の記述はない。 == 祀る神社 == * 鳴海杻神社 （[[愛知県]][[犬山市]]羽黒成海郷） - 主祭神 * 山岬神社（[[島根県]][[雲南市]][[大東町 (島根県)|大東町]]中湯石） - 祭神 ** 日原神社の境内社。 * [[神足神社]]（京都府長岡京市東神足） ** 現在の祭神は天神足命だが、[[本居宣長]]は本来の祭神を遠山岬多良斯津神とする。
== 参考文献 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[出雲族]] == 外部リンク == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/totsuyamasakitarashinokami/ 遠津山岬多良斯神 – 國學院大學 古典文化学事業] * [https://inuyama-tabi.com/point/detail/223/ 鳴海杻神社｜犬山文化遺産ナビ] * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/unnan/4af55d34f0d2979455af525d5f9769b71dee03dc.html 日原神社 - 神社詳細 | 島根県神社庁] * [https://nagaokatenmangu.or.jp/?page_id=113 兼務神社 - 長岡天満宮]（神足神社の詳細あり） {{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とおつやまさきたらしのかみ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:十七世神]]

== 参考文献 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[出雲族]] == 外部リンク == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/totsuyamasakitarashinokami/ 遠津山岬多良斯神 – 國學院大學 古典文化学事業] * [https://inuyama-tabi.com/point/detail/223/ 鳴海杻神社｜犬山文化遺産ナビ] * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/unnan/4af55d34f0d2979455af525d5f9769b71dee03dc.html 日原神社 - 神社詳細 | 島根県神社庁] * [https://nagaokatenmangu.or.jp/?page_id=113 兼務神社 - 長岡天満宮]（神足神社の詳細あり） {{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とおつやまさきたらしのかみ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:十七世神]]

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 遠津山岬多良斯神 | 世代名 = [[十七世神]] 第十五代 | 先代 = [[天日腹大科度美神]] | 次代 = | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 遠津山岬多良斯神（トオツヤマサキタラシノカミ） | 別名 = 遠津山岬帯神 | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの -->農業神 | 陵所 = | 父 = 天日腹大科度美神 | 母 = [[遠津待根神]] | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = 記載なし | 宮 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * 鳴海杻神社 * 山岬神社 等 }} | 記紀等 = [[古事記]] }} '''遠津山岬多良斯神'''（トオツヤマサキタラシ、{{smaller|[[歴史的仮名遣]]：}}トホツヤマサキタラシ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]。 == 概要 == 『古事記』にのみ登場する神で、[[十七世神]]（とおまりななよのかみ）の最後の神。初代[[八島士奴美神]]の14世孫にあたる。名称は他に'''遠津山岬帯神'''とも表記される。
「遠津」は「遠方の」、「山岬」は「山の崎」、「多良斯」は「足らし」で、名義は「遠方の山の崎が満ち足りていること」と考えられる。まず「遠津」は母方の遠津待根神から承けたもので、「山岬」は山の先端を指し、そこは台地であり、山からの水が台地を潤すため穀倉地帯を形成する。「多良斯」は充足を意味し、これによって上記のような名義と解される{{Sfn|西宮|2014|p=384}}。 == 系譜 == [[画像:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|大国主の系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神、黄は性別不詳]] [[大国主神]]の8世孫にあたる天日腹大科度美神が、[[天之狭霧神]]の娘である遠津待根神を娶って生んだ神。十七世神の最後であり、古事記にこれ以降の系譜の記述はない。 == 祀る神社 == * 鳴海杻神社 （[[愛知県]][[犬山市]]羽黒成海郷） - 主祭神 * 山岬神社（[[島根県]][[雲南市]][[大東町 (島根県)|大東町]]中湯石） - 祭神{{Unbulleted list|日原神社の境内社。}} * [[神足神社]]（京都府長岡京市東神足）{{Unbulleted list|現在の祭神は天神足命だが、[[本居宣長]]は本来の祭神を遠山岬多良斯津神とする。}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * <!-- にしみや -->{{Cite book|和書 |title=古事記 |volume= |date=2014年10月30日 |publisher=[[新潮社]] |ref={{SfnRef|西宮|2014}} |author=[[西宮一民]] 校注 |isbn=978-4-10-620801-0 |series=[[新潮日本古典集成]]（新装版）}} == 関連項目 == * [[大山津見神]] - 天之狭霧神の父。遠津山岬多良斯神から見て[[曽祖父]]に当たる。 == 外部リンク == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/totsuyamasakitarashinokami/ 遠津山岬多良斯神 – 國學院大學 古典文化学事業] * [https://inuyama-tabi.com/point/detail/223/ 鳴海杻神社｜犬山文化遺産ナビ] * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/unnan/4af55d34f0d2979455af525d5f9769b71dee03dc.html 日原神社 - 神社詳細 | 島根県神社庁] * [https://nagaokatenmangu.or.jp/?page_id=113 兼務神社 - 長岡天満宮]（神足神社の詳細あり） {{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とおつやまさきたらしのかみ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:十七世神]]

revision.comment: 6 revisions imported: importing my simple english wikipedia drafts for the 17 spirits of the world
revision.text:
{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = }}[[ja:遠津山岬多良斯神]] {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}} is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}.
He is the last of the 17 spirits of the world. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
His descendants are not recorded after this.
== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|en}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{Interlanguage link|Konohanachiruhime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto"> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>...
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shrine)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shrine)}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yaso Gods|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka Kami}} and had {{ill|Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.

This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}.

His descendants are not recorded after this.

== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|en}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{Interlanguage link|Konohanachiruhime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto"> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>...
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shrine)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shrine)}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yaso Gods|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka Kami}} and had {{ill|Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonumamashinomaouhime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonumamashinomaouhime}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}}.
== Family Tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories|[[Category:recently imported simple english 17 spirits of the world]] {{ill|Category:17 spirits of the world|ja|Category:十七世神|fr|Category:17 esprits du monde}} [[Category:Kunitsukami]] }}

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = }}[[ja:遠津山岬多良斯神]] {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}} is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}.
He is the last of the 17 spirits of the world. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
His descendants are not recorded after this.
== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|en}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.

This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = }}[[ja:遠津山岬多良斯神]] {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}} is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}.
He is the last of the 17 spirits of the world. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
His descendants are not recorded after this.

This god married {{Interlanguage link|Konohanachiruhime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto"> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>...

This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shrine)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shrine)}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yaso Gods|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka Kami}} and had {{ill|Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonumamashinomaouhime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonumamashinomaouhime}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami}}. [[ja:遠津山岬多良斯神]] This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}}.

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = }}[[ja:遠津山岬多良斯神]] {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}} is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}.
He is the last of the 17 spirits of the world. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
His descendants are not recorded after this.

(daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>...

This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shrine)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shrine)}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yaso Gods|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka Kami}} and had {{ill|Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonumamashinomaouhime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonumamashinomaouhime}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami}}. [[ja:遠津山岬多良斯神]] This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}}.
== Family Tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|user:immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神}} | type = Shinto | not_children = | not_father = | not_consort = | not_mother =
| Name = Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami | Generation = {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神}} 15th generation | Predecessor = {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神}} | Successor = None | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | Full Name = 遠津山岬多良斯神 (Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami) | Alternate Name= 遠津山岬帯神 | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = Agricultural deity | Mausoleum = | father = Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami | mother = {{ill|Totsumachine no Kami|ja|遠津待根神}} | Relatives = | consort = None recorded | children = None recorded | cult_centre = {{Plainlist| * Narumi-no-Ki Shrine * Yamamisaki Shrine }} | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} }}[[ja:遠津山岬多良斯神]]
'''Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami''' (遠津山岬多良斯神, トオツヤマサキタラシ) is a deity mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}. He is the last of the 17 spirits of the world. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
== Overview == Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami appears exclusively in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and is the final deity in the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神}} lineage. He is described as the 14th descendant of the first deity in this lineage, {{ill|Yashimajinumi|lt=Yashimajinumi no Kami|ja|八島士奴美神}}. His name is also recorded as '''Totsuyamasakiobi no Kami''' (遠津山岬帯神).
The name "Totsuyamasakitarashi" can be interpreted as: - "Totsu" (遠津): "Far-off" or "distant," inherited from his mother, Totsumachine no Kami. - "Yamasaki" (山岬): Refers to "mountain promontory," often associated with elevated fertile lands enriched by mountain waters. - "Tarashi" (多良斯): Implies "plenitude" or "sufficiency," symbolizing abundance and agricultural prosperity.{{Sfn|Nishimiya|2014|p=384}}
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (from the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]] Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami is the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神}}, himself a descendant of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}, and Totsumachine no Kami, a daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri no Kami|ja|天之狭霧神}}.
He is the last deity recorded in the ''Kojiki'' under the lineage of the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神}}, with no further genealogical information provided.
== Shrines Dedicated == * '''Narumi-no-Ki Shrine''' ({{ill|Inuyama|ja|犬山市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}) - Principal deity. * '''Yamamisaki Shrine''' ({{ill|Unnan|ja|雲南市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}) - Located within the grounds of {{ill|Nihara Shrine|ja|日原神社}} as an auxiliary shrine. * '''Kōtari Shrine''' ({{ill|Nagaokakyō|ja|長岡京市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}) - Currently enshrines {{ill|Amatsu Hikone|ja|天神足命}}, though {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}} identified the original deity as Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami.
== Notes == {{Reflist|group="Notes"}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=古事記|date=2014-10-30|publisher=[[Shinchōsha]]|editor=[[Nishimiya Kazutami|西宮一民]]|series=[[Shincho Japanese Classical Compendium]]|isbn=978-4-10-620801-0|ref={{SfnRef|Nishimiya|2014}}}}== See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神}} – The grandfather of Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami on his maternal side.
== External Links == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/totsuyamasakitarashinokami/ Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami – Kokugakuin University Classical Culture Project] * [https://inuyama-tabi.com/point/detail/223/ Narumi-no-Ki Shrine | Inuyama Cultural Heritage Navigator] * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/unnan/4af55d34f0d2979455af525d5f9769b71dee03dc.html Nihara Shrine | Shimane Shrine Association] * [https://nagaokatenmangu.or.jp/?page_id=113 Associated Shrines – Nagaoka Tenmangū] (details on Kōtari Shrine)

His name is also recorded as '''Totsuyamasakiobi no Kami''' (遠津山岬帯神).

== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (from the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]] Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami is the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神}}, himself a descendant of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}, and Totsumachine no Kami, a daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri no Kami|ja|天之狭霧神}}.
He is the last deity recorded in the ''Kojiki'' under the lineage of the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神}}, with no further genealogical information provided.

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|user:immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神}} | type = Shinto | not_children = | not_father = | not_consort = | not_mother =
| Name = Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami | Generation = {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神}} 15th generation | Predecessor = {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神}} | Successor = None | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | Full Name = 遠津山岬多良斯神 (Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami) | Alternate Name= 遠津山岬帯神 | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = Agricultural deity | Mausoleum = | father = Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami | mother = {{ill|Totsumachine no Kami|ja|遠津待根神}} | Relatives = | consort = None recorded | children = None recorded | cult_centre = {{Plainlist| * Narumi-no-Ki Shrine * Yamamisaki Shrine }} | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} }}[[ja:遠津山岬多良斯神]]
'''Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami''' (遠津山岬多良斯神, トオツヤマサキタラシ) is a deity mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}. He is the last of the 17 spirits of the world. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
== Overview == Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami appears exclusively in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and is the final deity in the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神}} lineage. He is described as the 14th descendant of the first deity in this lineage, {{ill|Yashimajinumi|lt=Yashimajinumi no Kami|ja|八島士奴美神|1=Yashimajinumi}}. His name is also recorded as '''Totsuyamasakiobi no Kami''' (遠津山岬帯神).
The name "Totsuyamasakitarashi" can be interpreted as: - "Totsu" (遠津): "Far-off" or "distant," inherited from his mother, Totsumachine no Kami. - "Yamasaki" (山岬): Refers to "mountain promontory," often associated with elevated fertile lands enriched by mountain waters. - "Tarashi" (多良斯): Implies "plenitude" or "sufficiency," symbolizing abundance and agricultural prosperity.{{Sfn|Nishimiya|2014|p=384}}
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (from the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]] Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami is the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神}}, himself a descendant of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}, and Totsumachine no Kami, a daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri no Kami|ja|天之狭霧神}}.
He is the last deity recorded in the ''Kojiki'' under the lineage of the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神}}, with no further genealogical information provided.
== Shrines Dedicated == * '''Narumi-no-Ki Shrine''' ({{ill|Inuyama|ja|犬山市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}) ** Principal deity. * '''Yamamisaki Shrine''' ({{ill|Unnan|ja|雲南市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}) ** Located within the grounds of {{ill|Nihara Shrine|ja|日原神社}} as an auxiliary shrine. * '''Kōtari Shrine''' ({{ill|Nagaokakyō|ja|長岡京市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}) ** Currently enshrines {{ill|Amatsu Hikone|ja|天神足命}}, though {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}} identified the original deity as Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami.
== Notes == {{Reflist|group="Notes"}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=古事記|date=2014-10-30|publisher=[[Shinchōsha]]|editor=[[Nishimiya Kazutami|西宮一民]]|series=[[Shincho Japanese Classical Compendium]]|isbn=978-4-10-620801-0|ref={{SfnRef|Nishimiya|2014}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神}} – The grandfather of Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami on his maternal side.
== External Links == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/totsuyamasakitarashinokami/ Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami – Kokugakuin University Classical Culture Project] * [https://inuyama-tabi.com/point/detail/223/ Narumi-no-Ki Shrine | Inuyama Cultural Heritage Navigator] * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/unnan/4af55d34f0d2979455af525d5f9769b71dee03dc.html Nihara Shrine | Shimane Shrine Association] * [https://nagaokatenmangu.or.jp/?page_id=113 Associated Shrines – Nagaoka Tenmangū] (details on Kōtari Shrine)

The name "Totsuyamasakitarashi" can be interpreted as: - "Totsu" (遠津): "Far-off" or "distant," inherited from his mother, Totsumachine no Kami. - "Yamasaki" (山岬): Refers to "mountain promontory," often associated with elevated fertile lands enriched by mountain waters. - "Tarashi" (多良斯): Implies "plenitude" or "sufficiency," symbolizing abundance and agricultural prosperity.{{Sfn|Nishimiya|2014|p=384}}
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (from the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]] Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami is the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神}}, himself a descendant of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}, and Totsumachine no Kami, a daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri no Kami|ja|天之狭霧神}}.
He is the last deity recorded in the ''Kojiki'' under the lineage of the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神}}, with no further genealogical information provided.

His name is also recorded as '''Totsuyamasakiobi no Kami''' (遠津山岬帯神).

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 遠津山岬多良斯神 | 世代名 = [[十七世神]] 第十五代 | 先代 = [[天日腹大科度美神]] | 次代 = | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 遠津山岬多良斯神（トオツヤマサキタラシノカミ） | 別名 = 遠津山岬帯神 | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの -->農業神 | 陵所 = | 父 = 天日腹大科度美神 | 母 = [[遠津待根神]] | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = 記載なし | 宮 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * 鳴海杻神社 * 山岬神社 等 }} | 記紀等 = [[古事記]] }} '''遠津山岬多良斯神'''（トオツヤマサキタラシ、{{smaller|[[歴史的仮名遣]]：}}トホツヤマサキタラシ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]。 == 概要 == 『古事記』にのみ登場する神で、[[十七世神]]（とおまりななよのかみ）の最後の神。初代[[八島士奴美神]]の14世孫にあたる。名称は他に'''遠津山岬帯神'''とも表記される。
「遠津」は「遠方の」、「山岬」は「山の崎」、「多良斯」は「足らし」で、名義は「遠方の山の崎が満ち足りていること」と考えられる。まず「遠津」は母方の遠津待根神から承けたもので、「山岬」は山の先端を指し、そこは台地であり、山からの水が台地を潤すため穀倉地帯を形成する。「多良斯」は充足を意味し、これによって上記のような名義と解される{{Sfn|西宮|2014|p=384}}。 == 系譜 == [[画像:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|大国主の系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神、黄は性別不詳]] [[大国主神]]の8世孫にあたる天日腹大科度美神が、[[天之狭霧神]]の娘である遠津待根神を娶って生んだ神。十七世神の最後であり、古事記にこれ以降の系譜の記述はない。 == 祀る神社 == * 鳴海杻神社 （[[愛知県]][[犬山市]]羽黒成海郷） - 主祭神 * 山岬神社（[[島根県]][[雲南市]][[大東町 (島根県)|大東町]]中湯石） - 祭神{{Unbulleted list|日原神社の境内社。}} * [[神足神社]]（京都府長岡京市東神足）{{Unbulleted list|現在の祭神は天神足命だが、[[本居宣長]]は本来の祭神を遠山岬多良斯津神とする。}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * <!-- にしみや -->{{Cite book|和書 |title=古事記 |volume= |date=2014年10月30日 |publisher=[[新潮社]] |ref={{SfnRef|西宮|2014}} |author=[[西宮一民]] 校注 |isbn=978-4-10-620801-0 |series=[[新潮日本古典集成]]（新装版）}} == 関連項目 == * [[大山津見神]] - 天之狭霧神の父。遠津山岬多良斯神から見て[[曽祖父]]に当たる。 == 外部リンク == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/totsuyamasakitarashinokami/ 遠津山岬多良斯神 – 國學院大學 古典文化学事業] * [https://inuyama-tabi.com/point/detail/223/ 鳴海杻神社｜犬山文化遺産ナビ] * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/unnan/4af55d34f0d2979455af525d5f9769b71dee03dc.html 日原神社 - 神社詳細 | 島根県神社庁] * [https://nagaokatenmangu.or.jp/?page_id=113 兼務神社 - 長岡天満宮]（神足神社の詳細あり） {{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とおつやまさきたらしのかみ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:十七世神]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:遠津山岬多良斯神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q55523289]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|qq=}} | type = Shinto | not_children = | not_father = | not_consort = | not_mother =
| Name = Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami | Generation = {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}} 15th generation | Predecessor = {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}} | Successor = None | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | Full Name = 遠津山岬多良斯神 (Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami) | Alternate Name= 遠津山岬帯神 | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = Agricultural deity | Mausoleum = | father = Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami | mother = {{ill|Totsumachine no Kami|ja|遠津待根神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsumachine no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Relatives = | consort = None recorded | children = None recorded | cult_centre = {{Plainlist| * Narumi-no-Ki Shrine * Yamamisaki Shrine }} | Texts = [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] }}
'''Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami''' (遠津山岬多良斯神, トオツヤマサキタラシ) is a deity mentioned in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. He is the last of the 17 spirits of the world. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
== Overview == Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami appears exclusively in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' and is the final deity in the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}} lineage. He is described as the 14th descendant of the first deity in this lineage, [[:en:Yashimajinumi|Yashimajinumi no Kami]]. His name is also recorded as '''Totsuyamasakiobi no Kami''' (遠津山岬帯神).
The name "Totsuyamasakitarashi" can be interpreted as: - "Totsu" (遠津): "Far-off" or "distant," inherited from his mother, Totsumachine no Kami. - "Yamasaki" (山岬): Refers to "mountain promontory," often associated with elevated fertile lands enriched by mountain waters. - "Tarashi" (多良斯): Implies "plenitude" or "sufficiency," symbolizing abundance and agricultural prosperity.{{Sfn|Nishimiya|2014|p=384}}
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (from the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]] Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami is the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}}, himself a descendant of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and Totsumachine no Kami, a daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri no Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}.
He is the last deity recorded in the ''Kojiki'' under the lineage of the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, with no further genealogical information provided.
== Shrines Dedicated == * '''Narumi-no-Ki Shrine''' ([[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]) ** Principal deity. * '''Yamamisaki Shrine''' ([[:en:Unnan|Unnan]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]) ** Located within the grounds of {{ill|Nihara Shrine|ja|日原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as an auxiliary shrine. * '''Kōtari Shrine''' ([[:en:Nagaokakyō, Kyoto|Nagaokakyō]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]) ** Currently enshrines {{ill|Amatsu Hikone|ja|天神足命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu Hikone|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, though [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] identified the original deity as Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami.
== Notes == {{Reflist|group="Notes"}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=古事記|date=2014-10-30|publisher=[[Shinchōsha]]|editor=[[Nishimiya Kazutami|西宮一民]]|series=[[Shincho Japanese Classical Compendium]]|isbn=978-4-10-620801-0|ref={{SfnRef|Nishimiya|2014}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} – The grandfather of Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami on his maternal side.
== External Links == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/totsuyamasakitarashinokami/ Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami – Kokugakuin University Classical Culture Project] * [https://inuyama-tabi.com/point/detail/223/ Narumi-no-Ki Shrine | Inuyama Cultural Heritage Navigator] * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/unnan/4af55d34f0d2979455af525d5f9769b71dee03dc.html Nihara Shrine | Shimane Shrine Association] * [https://nagaokatenmangu.or.jp/?page_id=113 Associated Shrines – Nagaoka Tenmangū] (details on Kōtari Shrine)

| Name = Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami | Generation = {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}} 15th generation | Predecessor = {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}} | Successor = None | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | Full Name = 遠津山岬多良斯神 (Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami) | Alternate Name= 遠津山岬帯神 | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = Agricultural deity | Mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|WD=Q60996386}} | mother = {{ill|Totsumachine no Kami|ja|遠津待根神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsumachine no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135330329|1=Totsumatone-no-Kami}} | Relatives = | consort = None recorded | children = None recorded | cult_centre = {{Plainlist| * Narumi-no-Ki Shrine * Yamamisaki Shrine }} | Texts = [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] }}
'''Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami''' (遠津山岬多良斯神, トオツヤマサキタラシ) is a deity mentioned in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. He is the last of the 17 spirits of the world. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
== Overview == Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami appears exclusively in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' and is the final deity in the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}} lineage. He is described as the 14th descendant of the first deity in this lineage, [[:en:Yashimajinumi|Yashimajinumi no Kami]]. His name is also recorded as '''Totsuyamasakiobi no Kami''' (遠津山岬帯神).
The name "Totsuyamasakitarashi" can be interpreted as: - "Totsu" (遠津): "Far-off" or "distant," inherited from his mother, Totsumachine no Kami. - "Yamasaki" (山岬): Refers to "mountain promontory," often associated with elevated fertile lands enriched by mountain waters. - "Tarashi" (多良斯): Implies "plenitude" or "sufficiency," symbolizing abundance and agricultural prosperity.{{Sfn|Nishimiya|2014|p=384}}
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (from the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]] Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami is the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}}, himself a descendant of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and Totsumachine no Kami, a daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri no Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}.
He is the last deity recorded in the ''Kojiki'' under the lineage of the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, with no further genealogical information provided.
== Shrines Dedicated == * '''Narumi-no-Ki Shrine''' ([[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]) ** Principal deity. * '''Yamamisaki Shrine''' ([[:en:Unnan|Unnan]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]) ** Located within the grounds of {{ill|Nihara Shrine|ja|日原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as an auxiliary shrine. * '''Kōtari Shrine''' ([[:en:Nagaokakyō, Kyoto|Nagaokakyō]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]) ** Currently enshrines {{ill|Amatsu Hikone|ja|天神足命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu Hikone|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, though [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] identified the original deity as Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami.

| Name = Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami | Generation = {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}} 15th generation | Predecessor = {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}} | Successor = None | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | Full Name = 遠津山岬多良斯神 (Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami) | Alternate Name= 遠津山岬帯神 | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = Agricultural deity | Mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|WD=Q60996386}} | mother = {{ill|Totsumachine no Kami|ja|遠津待根神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsumachine no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135330329|1=Totsumatone-no-Kami}} | Relatives = | consort = None recorded | children = None recorded | cult_centre = {{Plainlist| * Narumi-no-Ki Shrine * Yamamisaki Shrine }} | Texts = [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] }}
'''Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami''' (遠津山岬多良斯神, トオツヤマサキタラシ) is a deity mentioned in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. He is the last of the 17 spirits of the world. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
== Overview == Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami appears exclusively in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' and is the final deity in the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}} lineage. He is described as the 14th descendant of the first deity in this lineage, [[:en:Yashimajinumi|Yashimajinumi no Kami]]. His name is also recorded as '''Totsuyamasakiobi no Kami''' (遠津山岬帯神).
The name "Totsuyamasakitarashi" can be interpreted as: - "Totsu" (遠津): "Far-off" or "distant," inherited from his mother, Totsumachine no Kami. - "Yamasaki" (山岬): Refers to "mountain promontory," often associated with elevated fertile lands enriched by mountain waters. - "Tarashi" (多良斯): Implies "plenitude" or "sufficiency," symbolizing abundance and agricultural prosperity.{{Sfn|Nishimiya|2014|p=384}}
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (from the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]] Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami is the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}}, himself a descendant of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and Totsumachine no Kami, a daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri no Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}.
He is the last deity recorded in the ''Kojiki'' under the lineage of the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, with no further genealogical information provided.
== Shrines Dedicated == * '''Narumi-no-Ki Shrine''' ([[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]) ** Principal deity. * '''Yamamisaki Shrine''' ([[:en:Unnan|Unnan]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]) ** Located within the grounds of {{ill|Nihara Shrine|ja|日原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as an auxiliary shrine. * '''Kōtari Shrine''' ([[:en:Nagaokakyō, Kyoto|Nagaokakyō]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]) ** Currently enshrines {{ill|Amatsu Hikone|ja|天神足命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu Hikone|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, though [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] identified the original deity as Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami.
== Notes == {{Reflist|group="Notes"}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=古事記|date=2014-10-30|publisher=[[Shinchōsha]]|editor=[[Nishimiya Kazutami|西宮一民]]|series=[[Shincho Japanese Classical Compendium]]|isbn=978-4-10-620801-0|ref={{SfnRef|Nishimiya|2014}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} – The grandfather of Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami on his maternal side.
== External Links == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/totsuyamasakitarashinokami/ Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami – Kokugakuin University Classical Culture Project] * [https://inuyama-tabi.com/point/detail/223/ Narumi-no-Ki Shrine | Inuyama Cultural Heritage Navigator] * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/unnan/4af55d34f0d2979455af525d5f9769b71dee03dc.html Nihara Shrine | Shimane Shrine Association] * [https://nagaokatenmangu.or.jp/?page_id=113 Associated Shrines – Nagaoka Tenmangū] (details on Kōtari Shrine)

| Name = Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami | Generation = {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}} 15th generation | Predecessor = {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}} | Successor = None | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | Full Name = 遠津山岬多良斯神 (Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami) | Alternate Name= 遠津山岬帯神 | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = Agricultural deity | Mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|WD=Q60996386}} | mother = {{ill|Totsumachine no Kami|ja|遠津待根神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsumachine no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135330329|1=Totsumatone-no-Kami}} | Relatives = | consort = None recorded | children = None recorded | cult_centre = {{Plainlist| * Narumi-no-Ki Shrine * Yamamisaki Shrine }} | Texts = [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] }}
'''Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami''' (遠津山岬多良斯神, トオツヤマサキタラシ) is a deity mentioned in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. He is the last of the 17 spirits of the world. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
== Overview == Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami appears exclusively in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' and is the final deity in the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}} lineage. He is described as the 14th descendant of the first deity in this lineage, [[:en:Yashimajinumi|Yashimajinumi no Kami]]. His name is also recorded as '''Totsuyamasakiobi no Kami''' (遠津山岬帯神).
The name "Totsuyamasakitarashi" can be interpreted as: - "Totsu" (遠津): "Far-off" or "distant," inherited from his mother, Totsumachine no Kami. - "Yamasaki" (山岬): Refers to "mountain promontory," often associated with elevated fertile lands enriched by mountain waters. - "Tarashi" (多良斯): Implies "plenitude" or "sufficiency," symbolizing abundance and agricultural prosperity.{{Sfn|Nishimiya|2014|p=384}}
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (from the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]] Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami is the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}}, himself a descendant of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and Totsumachine no Kami, a daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri no Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}.
He is the last deity recorded in the ''Kojiki'' under the lineage of the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, with no further genealogical information provided.
== Shrines Dedicated == * '''Narumi-no-Ki Shrine''' ([[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]) ** Principal deity. * '''Yamamisaki Shrine''' ([[:en:Unnan|Unnan]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]) ** Located within the grounds of {{ill|Nihara Shrine|ja|日原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as an auxiliary shrine. * '''Kōtari Shrine''' ([[:en:Nagaokakyō, Kyoto|Nagaokakyō]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]) ** Currently enshrines {{ill|Amatsu Hikone|ja|天神足命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu Hikone|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, though [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] identified the original deity as Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami.

| Name = Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami | Generation = {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}} 15th generation | Predecessor = {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}} | Successor = None | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | Full Name = 遠津山岬多良斯神 (Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami) | Alternate Name= 遠津山岬帯神 | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = Agricultural deity | Mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|WD=Q60996386}} | mother = {{ill|Totsumachine no Kami|ja|遠津待根神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsumachine no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135330329|1=Totsumatone-no-Kami}} | Relatives = | consort = None recorded | children = None recorded | cult_centre = {{Plainlist| * Narumi-no-Ki Shrine * Yamamisaki Shrine }} | Texts = [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] }}
'''Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami''' (遠津山岬多良斯神, トオツヤマサキタラシ) is a deity mentioned in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. He is the last of the 17 spirits of the world. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
== Overview == Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami appears exclusively in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' and is the final deity in the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}} lineage. He is described as the 14th descendant of the first deity in this lineage, [[:en:Yashimajinumi|Yashimajinumi no Kami]]. His name is also recorded as '''Totsuyamasakiobi no Kami''' (遠津山岬帯神).
The name "Totsuyamasakitarashi" can be interpreted as: - "Totsu" (遠津): "Far-off" or "distant," inherited from his mother, Totsumachine no Kami. - "Yamasaki" (山岬): Refers to "mountain promontory," often associated with elevated fertile lands enriched by mountain waters. - "Tarashi" (多良斯): Implies "plenitude" or "sufficiency," symbolizing abundance and agricultural prosperity.{{Sfn|Nishimiya|2014|p=384}}
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (from the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]] Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami is the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}}, himself a descendant of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and Totsumachine no Kami, a daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri no Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}.
He is the last deity recorded in the ''Kojiki'' under the lineage of the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, with no further genealogical information provided.
== Shrines Dedicated == * '''Narumi-no-Ki Shrine''' ([[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]) ** Principal deity. * '''Yamamisaki Shrine''' ([[:en:Unnan|Unnan]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]) ** Located within the grounds of {{ill|Nihara Shrine|ja|日原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as an auxiliary shrine. * '''Kōtari Shrine''' ([[:en:Nagaokakyō, Kyoto|Nagaokakyō]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]) ** Currently enshrines {{ill|Amatsu Hikone|ja|天神足命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu Hikone|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, though [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] identified the original deity as Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami.
== Notes == {{Reflist|group="Notes"}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=古事記|date=2014-10-30|publisher=[[Shinchōsha]]|editor=[[Nishimiya Kazutami|西宮一民]]|series=[[Shincho Japanese Classical Compendium]]|isbn=978-4-10-620801-0|ref={{SfnRef|Nishimiya|2014}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} – The grandfather of Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami on his maternal side.
== External Links == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/totsuyamasakitarashinokami/ Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami – Kokugakuin University Classical Culture Project] * [https://inuyama-tabi.com/point/detail/223/ Narumi-no-Ki Shrine | Inuyama Cultural Heritage Navigator] * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/unnan/4af55d34f0d2979455af525d5f9769b71dee03dc.html Nihara Shrine | Shimane Shrine Association] * [https://nagaokatenmangu.or.jp/?page_id=113 Associated Shrines – Nagaoka Tenmangū] (details on Kōtari Shrine)
