| Name = Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami | Generation = {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}} 15th generation | Predecessor = {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}} | Successor = None | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | Full Name = 遠津山岬多良斯神 (Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami) | Alternate Name= 遠津山岬帯神 | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = Agricultural deity | Mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|WD=Q60996386}} | mother = {{ill|Totsumachine no Kami|ja|遠津待根神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsumachine no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135330329|1=Totsumatone-no-Kami}} | Relatives = | consort = None recorded | children = None recorded | cult_centre = {{Plainlist| * Narumi-no-Ki Shrine * Yamamisaki Shrine }} | Texts = [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] }}
'''Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami''' (遠津山岬多良斯神, トオツヤマサキタラシ) is a deity mentioned in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. He is the last of the 17 spirits of the world. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
== Overview == Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami appears exclusively in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' and is the final deity in the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}} lineage. He is described as the 14th descendant of the first deity in this lineage, [[:en:Yashimajinumi|Yashimajinumi no Kami]]. His name is also recorded as '''Totsuyamasakiobi no Kami''' (遠津山岬帯神).
The name "Totsuyamasakitarashi" can be interpreted as: - "Totsu" (遠津): "Far-off" or "distant," inherited from his mother, Totsumachine no Kami. - "Yamasaki" (山岬): Refers to "mountain promontory," often associated with elevated fertile lands enriched by mountain waters. - "Tarashi" (多良斯): Implies "plenitude" or "sufficiency," symbolizing abundance and agricultural prosperity.{{Sfn|Nishimiya|2014|p=384}}
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (from the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]] Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami is the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}}, himself a descendant of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and Totsumachine no Kami, a daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri no Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}.
He is the last deity recorded in the ''Kojiki'' under the lineage of the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, with no further genealogical information provided.
== Shrines Dedicated == * '''Narumi-no-Ki Shrine''' ([[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]) ** Principal deity. * '''Yamamisaki Shrine''' ([[:en:Unnan|Unnan]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]) ** Located within the grounds of {{ill|Nihara Shrine|ja|日原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as an auxiliary shrine. * '''Kōtari Shrine''' ([[:en:Nagaokakyō, Kyoto|Nagaokakyō]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]) ** Currently enshrines {{ill|Amatsu Hikone|ja|天神足命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu Hikone|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, though [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] identified the original deity as Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami.
== Notes == {{Reflist|group="Notes"}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=古事記|date=2014-10-30|publisher=[[Shinchōsha]]|editor=[[Nishimiya Kazutami|西宮一民]]|series=[[Shincho Japanese Classical Compendium]]|isbn=978-4-10-620801-0|ref={{SfnRef|Nishimiya|2014}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} – The grandfather of Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami on his maternal side.
== External Links == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/totsuyamasakitarashinokami/ Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami – Kokugakuin University Classical Culture Project] * [https://inuyama-tabi.com/point/detail/223/ Narumi-no-Ki Shrine | Inuyama Cultural Heritage Navigator] * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/unnan/4af55d34f0d2979455af525d5f9769b71dee03dc.html Nihara Shrine | Shimane Shrine Association] * [https://nagaokatenmangu.or.jp/?page_id=113 Associated Shrines – Nagaoka Tenmangū] (details on Kōtari Shrine)
{{Japanese mythology}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} == Family Tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}

| Name = Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami | Generation = {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}} 15th generation | Predecessor = {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}} | Successor = None | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | Full Name = 遠津山岬多良斯神 (Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami) | Alternate Name= 遠津山岬帯神 | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = Agricultural deity | Mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|WD=Q60996386}} | mother = {{ill|Totsumachine no Kami|ja|遠津待根神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsumachine no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135330329|1=Totsumatone-no-Kami}} | Relatives = | consort = None recorded | children = None recorded | cult_centre = {{Plainlist| * Narumi-no-Ki Shrine * Yamamisaki Shrine }} | Texts = [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] }}
'''Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami''' (遠津山岬多良斯神, トオツヤマサキタラシ) is a deity mentioned in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. He is the last of the 17 spirits of the world. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
== Overview == Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami appears exclusively in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' and is the final deity in the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}} lineage. He is described as the 14th descendant of the first deity in this lineage, [[:en:Yashimajinumi|Yashimajinumi no Kami]]. His name is also recorded as '''Totsuyamasakiobi no Kami''' (遠津山岬帯神).
The name "Totsuyamasakitarashi" can be interpreted as: - "Totsu" (遠津): "Far-off" or "distant," inherited from his mother, Totsumachine no Kami. - "Yamasaki" (山岬): Refers to "mountain promontory," often associated with elevated fertile lands enriched by mountain waters. - "Tarashi" (多良斯): Implies "plenitude" or "sufficiency," symbolizing abundance and agricultural prosperity.{{Sfn|Nishimiya|2014|p=384}}
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (from the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]] Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami is the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}}, himself a descendant of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and Totsumachine no Kami, a daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri no Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}.
He is the last deity recorded in the ''Kojiki'' under the lineage of the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, with no further genealogical information provided.
== Shrines Dedicated == * '''Narumi-no-Ki Shrine''' ([[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]) ** Principal deity. * '''Yamamisaki Shrine''' ([[:en:Unnan|Unnan]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]) ** Located within the grounds of {{ill|Nihara Shrine|ja|日原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as an auxiliary shrine. * '''Kōtari Shrine''' ([[:en:Nagaokakyō, Kyoto|Nagaokakyō]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]) ** Currently enshrines {{ill|Amatsu Hikone|ja|天神足命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu Hikone|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, though [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] identified the original deity as Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami.
== Notes == {{Reflist|group="Notes"}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=古事記|date=2014-10-30|publisher=[[Shinchōsha]]|editor=[[Nishimiya Kazutami|西宮一民]]|series=[[Shincho Japanese Classical Compendium]]|isbn=978-4-10-620801-0|ref={{SfnRef|Nishimiya|2014}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} – The grandfather of Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami on his maternal side.
== External Links == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/totsuyamasakitarashinokami/ Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami – Kokugakuin University Classical Culture Project] * [https://inuyama-tabi.com/point/detail/223/ Narumi-no-Ki Shrine | Inuyama Cultural Heritage Navigator] * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/unnan/4af55d34f0d2979455af525d5f9769b71dee03dc.html Nihara Shrine | Shimane Shrine Association] * [https://nagaokatenmangu.or.jp/?page_id=113 Associated Shrines – Nagaoka Tenmangū] (details on Kōtari Shrine)

| Name = Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami | Generation = {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}} 15th generation | Predecessor = {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}} | Successor = None | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | Full Name = 遠津山岬多良斯神 (Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami) | Alternate Name= 遠津山岬帯神 | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = Agricultural deity | Mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|WD=Q60996386}} | mother = {{ill|Totsumachine no Kami|ja|遠津待根神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsumachine no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135330329|1=Totsumatone-no-Kami}} | Relatives = | consort = None recorded | children = None recorded | cult_centre = {{Plainlist| * Narumi-no-Ki Shrine * Yamamisaki Shrine }} | Texts = [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] }}
'''Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami''' (遠津山岬多良斯神, トオツヤマサキタラシ) is a deity mentioned in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. He is the last of the 17 spirits of the world. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
== Overview == Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami appears exclusively in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' and is the final deity in the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}} lineage. He is described as the 14th descendant of the first deity in this lineage, [[:en:Yashimajinumi|Yashimajinumi no Kami]]. His name is also recorded as '''Totsuyamasakiobi no Kami''' (遠津山岬帯神).
The name "Totsuyamasakitarashi" can be interpreted as: - "Totsu" (遠津): "Far-off" or "distant," inherited from his mother, Totsumachine no Kami. - "Yamasaki" (山岬): Refers to "mountain promontory," often associated with elevated fertile lands enriched by mountain waters. - "Tarashi" (多良斯): Implies "plenitude" or "sufficiency," symbolizing abundance and agricultural prosperity.{{Sfn|Nishimiya|2014|p=384}}
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (from the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]] Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami is the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}}, himself a descendant of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and Totsumachine no Kami, a daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri no Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}.
He is the last deity recorded in the ''Kojiki'' under the lineage of the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, with no further genealogical information provided.
== Shrines Dedicated == * '''Narumi-no-Ki Shrine''' ([[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]) ** Principal deity. * '''Yamamisaki Shrine''' ([[:en:Unnan|Unnan]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]) ** Located within the grounds of {{ill|Nihara Shrine|ja|日原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as an auxiliary shrine. * '''Kōtari Shrine''' ([[:en:Nagaokakyō, Kyoto|Nagaokakyō]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]) ** Currently enshrines {{ill|Amatsu Hikone|ja|天神足命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu Hikone|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, though [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] identified the original deity as Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami.
== Notes == {{Reflist|group="Notes"}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=古事記|date=2014-10-30|publisher=[[Shinchōsha]]|editor=[[Nishimiya Kazutami|西宮一民]]|series=[[Shincho Japanese Classical Compendium]]|isbn=978-4-10-620801-0|ref={{SfnRef|Nishimiya|2014}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} – The grandfather of Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami on his maternal side.
== External Links == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/totsuyamasakitarashinokami/ Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami – Kokugakuin University Classical Culture Project] * [https://inuyama-tabi.com/point/detail/223/ Narumi-no-Ki Shrine | Inuyama Cultural Heritage Navigator] * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/unnan/4af55d34f0d2979455af525d5f9769b71dee03dc.html Nihara Shrine | Shimane Shrine Association] * [https://nagaokatenmangu.or.jp/?page_id=113 Associated Shrines – Nagaoka Tenmangū] (details on Kōtari Shrine)

| Name = Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami | Generation = {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}} 15th generation | Predecessor = {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}} | Successor = None | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | Full Name = 遠津山岬多良斯神 (Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami) | Alternate Name= 遠津山岬帯神 | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = Agricultural deity | Mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|WD=Q60996386}} | mother = {{ill|Totsumachine no Kami|ja|遠津待根神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsumachine no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135330329|1=Totsumatone-no-Kami}} | Relatives = | consort = None recorded | children = None recorded | cult_centre = {{Plainlist| * Narumi-no-Ki Shrine * Yamamisaki Shrine }} | Texts = [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] }}
'''Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami''' (遠津山岬多良斯神, トオツヤマサキタラシ) is a deity mentioned in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. He is the last of the 17 spirits of the world. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
== Overview == Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami appears exclusively in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' and is the final deity in the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}} lineage. He is described as the 14th descendant of the first deity in this lineage, [[:en:Yashimajinumi|Yashimajinumi no Kami]]. His name is also recorded as '''Totsuyamasakiobi no Kami''' (遠津山岬帯神).
The name "Totsuyamasakitarashi" can be interpreted as: - "Totsu" (遠津): "Far-off" or "distant," inherited from his mother, Totsumachine no Kami. - "Yamasaki" (山岬): Refers to "mountain promontory," often associated with elevated fertile lands enriched by mountain waters. - "Tarashi" (多良斯): Implies "plenitude" or "sufficiency," symbolizing abundance and agricultural prosperity.{{Sfn|Nishimiya|2014|p=384}}
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (from the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]] Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami is the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}}, himself a descendant of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and Totsumachine no Kami, a daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri no Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}.
He is the last deity recorded in the ''Kojiki'' under the lineage of the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, with no further genealogical information provided.
== Shrines Dedicated == * '''Narumi-no-Ki Shrine''' ([[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]) ** Principal deity. * '''Yamamisaki Shrine''' ([[:en:Unnan|Unnan]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]) ** Located within the grounds of {{ill|Nihara Shrine|ja|日原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as an auxiliary shrine. * '''Kōtari Shrine''' ([[:en:Nagaokakyō, Kyoto|Nagaokakyō]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]) ** Currently enshrines {{ill|Amatsu Hikone|ja|天神足命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu Hikone|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, though [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] identified the original deity as Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami.
== Notes == {{Reflist|group="Notes"}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=古事記|date=2014-10-30|publisher=[[Shinchōsha]]|editor=[[Nishimiya Kazutami|西宮一民]]|series=[[Shincho Japanese Classical Compendium]]|isbn=978-4-10-620801-0|ref={{SfnRef|Nishimiya|2014}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} – The grandfather of Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami on his maternal side.
== External Links == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/totsuyamasakitarashinokami/ Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami – Kokugakuin University Classical Culture Project] * [https://inuyama-tabi.com/point/detail/223/ Narumi-no-Ki Shrine | Inuyama Cultural Heritage Navigator] * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/unnan/4af55d34f0d2979455af525d5f9769b71dee03dc.html Nihara Shrine | Shimane Shrine Association] * [https://nagaokatenmangu.or.jp/?page_id=113 Associated Shrines – Nagaoka Tenmangū] (details on Kōtari Shrine)

| Name = Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami | Generation = {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}} 15th generation | Predecessor = {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}} | Successor = None | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | Full Name = 遠津山岬多良斯神 (Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami) | Alternate Name= 遠津山岬帯神 | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = Agricultural deity | Mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|WD=Q60996386}} | mother = {{ill|Totsumachine no Kami|ja|遠津待根神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsumachine no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135330329|1=Totsumatone-no-Kami}} | Relatives = | consort = None recorded | children = None recorded | cult_centre = {{Plainlist| * Narumi-no-Ki Shrine * Yamamisaki Shrine }} | Texts = [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] }}
'''Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami''' (遠津山岬多良斯神, トオツヤマサキタラシ) is a deity mentioned in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. He is the last of the 17 spirits of the world. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
== Overview == Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami appears exclusively in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' and is the final deity in the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}} lineage. He is described as the 14th descendant of the first deity in this lineage, [[:en:Yashimajinumi|Yashimajinumi no Kami]]. His name is also recorded as '''Totsuyamasakiobi no Kami''' (遠津山岬帯神).
The name "Totsuyamasakitarashi" can be interpreted as: - "Totsu" (遠津): "Far-off" or "distant," inherited from his mother, Totsumachine no Kami. - "Yamasaki" (山岬): Refers to "mountain promontory," often associated with elevated fertile lands enriched by mountain waters. - "Tarashi" (多良斯): Implies "plenitude" or "sufficiency," symbolizing abundance and agricultural prosperity.{{Sfn|Nishimiya|2014|p=384}}
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (from the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]] Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami is the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}}, himself a descendant of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and Totsumachine no Kami, a daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri no Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}.
He is the last deity recorded in the ''Kojiki'' under the lineage of the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, with no further genealogical information provided.
== Shrines Dedicated == * '''Narumi-no-Ki Shrine''' ([[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]) ** Principal deity. * '''Yamamisaki Shrine''' ([[:en:Unnan|Unnan]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]) ** Located within the grounds of {{ill|Nihara Shrine|ja|日原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as an auxiliary shrine. * '''Kōtari Shrine''' ([[:en:Nagaokakyō, Kyoto|Nagaokakyō]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]) ** Currently enshrines {{ill|Amatsu Hikone|ja|天神足命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu Hikone|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, though [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] identified the original deity as Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami.
== Notes == {{Reflist|group="Notes"}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=古事記|date=2014-10-30|publisher=[[Shinchōsha]]|editor=[[Nishimiya Kazutami|西宮一民]]|series=[[Shincho Japanese Classical Compendium]]|isbn=978-4-10-620801-0|ref={{SfnRef|Nishimiya|2014}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} – The grandfather of Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami on his maternal side.
== External Links == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/totsuyamasakitarashinokami/ Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami – Kokugakuin University Classical Culture Project] * [https://inuyama-tabi.com/point/detail/223/ Narumi-no-Ki Shrine | Inuyama Cultural Heritage Navigator] * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/unnan/4af55d34f0d2979455af525d5f9769b71dee03dc.html Nihara Shrine | Shimane Shrine Association] * [https://nagaokatenmangu.or.jp/?page_id=113 Associated Shrines – Nagaoka Tenmangū] (details on Kōtari Shrine)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:遠津山岬多良斯神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:遠津山岬多良斯神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:遠津山岬多良斯神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55523289]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|qq=}} | type = Shinto | not_children = | not_father = | not_consort = | not_mother =
| Name = Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami | Generation = {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}} 15th generation | Predecessor = {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}} | Successor = None | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | Full Name = 遠津山岬多良斯神 (Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami) | Alternate Name= 遠津山岬帯神 | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = Agricultural deity | Mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|WD=Q60996386}} | mother = {{ill|Totsumachine no Kami|ja|遠津待根神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsumachine no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135330329|1=Totsumatone-no-Kami}} | Relatives = | consort = None recorded | children = None recorded | cult_centre = {{Plainlist| * Narumi-no-Ki Shrine * Yamamisaki Shrine }} | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}} }}
'''Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami''' (遠津山岬多良斯神, トオツヤマサキタラシ) is a deity mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}. He is the last of the 17 spirits of the world. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
== Overview == Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami appears exclusively in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'' and is the final deity in the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}} lineage. He is described as the 14th descendant of the first deity in this lineage, {{ill|Yashimajinumi|en|Yashimajinumi|WD=Q55522907|lt=Yashimajinumi no Kami}}. His name is also recorded as '''Totsuyamasakiobi no Kami''' (遠津山岬帯神).
The name "Totsuyamasakitarashi" can be interpreted as: - "Totsu" (遠津): "Far-off" or "distant," inherited from his mother, Totsumachine no Kami. - "Yamasaki" (山岬): Refers to "mountain promontory," often associated with elevated fertile lands enriched by mountain waters. - "Tarashi" (多良斯): Implies "plenitude" or "sufficiency," symbolizing abundance and agricultural prosperity.{{Sfn|Nishimiya|2014|p=384}}
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (from the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]] Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami is the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}}, himself a descendant of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and Totsumachine no Kami, a daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri no Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}.
He is the last deity recorded in the ''Kojiki'' under the lineage of the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, with no further genealogical information provided.
== Shrines Dedicated == * '''Narumi-no-Ki Shrine''' ({{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|WD=Q503411|lt=Inuyama}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}) ** Principal deity. * '''Yamamisaki Shrine''' ({{ill|Unnan|en|Unnan|WD=Q877935|lt=Unnan}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}) ** Located within the grounds of {{ill|Nihara Shrine|ja|日原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as an auxiliary shrine. * '''Kōtari Shrine''' ({{ill|Nagaokakyō, Kyoto|en|Nagaokakyō, Kyoto|WD=Q861246|lt=Nagaokakyō}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}) ** Currently enshrines {{ill|Amatsu Hikone|ja|天神足命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu Hikone|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, though {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|en|Motoori Norinaga|WD=Q384647|lt=Motoori Norinaga}} identified the original deity as Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami.
== Notes == {{Reflist|group="Notes"}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=古事記|date=2014-10-30|publisher=[[Shinchōsha]]|editor=[[Nishimiya Kazutami|西宮一民]]|series=[[Shincho Japanese Classical Compendium]]|isbn=978-4-10-620801-0|ref={{SfnRef|Nishimiya|2014}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} – The grandfather of Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami on his maternal side.
== External Links == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/totsuyamasakitarashinokami/ Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami – Kokugakuin University Classical Culture Project] * [https://inuyama-tabi.com/point/detail/223/ Narumi-no-Ki Shrine | Inuyama Cultural Heritage Navigator] * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/unnan/4af55d34f0d2979455af525d5f9769b71dee03dc.html Nihara Shrine | Shimane Shrine Association] * [https://nagaokatenmangu.or.jp/?page_id=113 Associated Shrines – Nagaoka Tenmangū] (details on Kōtari Shrine)

revision.comment: Text replacement - "17 spirits of the world" to "Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:遠津山岬多良斯神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:遠津山岬多良斯神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:遠津山岬多良斯神]]
{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=The 17 Generations of Deities|lt=Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|simple|User:Immanuelle/Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|qq=}} | type = Shinto | not_children = | not_father = | not_consort = | not_mother =
| Name = Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami | Generation = {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}} 15th generation | Predecessor = {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}} | Successor = None | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | Full Name = 遠津山岬多良斯神 (Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami) | Alternate Name= 遠津山岬帯神 | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = Agricultural deity | Mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|WD=Q60996386}} | mother = {{ill|Totsumachine no Kami|ja|遠津待根神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsumachine no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135330329|1=Totsumatone-no-Kami}} | Relatives = | consort = None recorded | children = None recorded | cult_centre = {{Plainlist| * Narumi-no-Ki Shrine * Yamamisaki Shrine }} | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}} }}
'''Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami''' (遠津山岬多良斯神, トオツヤマサキタラシ) is a deity mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}. He is the last of the Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
== Overview == Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami appears exclusively in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'' and is the final deity in the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}} lineage. He is described as the 14th descendant of the first deity in this lineage, {{ill|Yashimajinumi|en|Yashimajinumi|WD=Q55522907|lt=Yashimajinumi no Kami}}. His name is also recorded as '''Totsuyamasakiobi no Kami''' (遠津山岬帯神).
The name "Totsuyamasakitarashi" can be interpreted as: - "Totsu" (遠津): "Far-off" or "distant," inherited from his mother, Totsumachine no Kami. - "Yamasaki" (山岬): Refers to "mountain promontory," often associated with elevated fertile lands enriched by mountain waters. - "Tarashi" (多良斯): Implies "plenitude" or "sufficiency," symbolizing abundance and agricultural prosperity.{{Sfn|Nishimiya|2014|p=384}}
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (from the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]] Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami is the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}}, himself a descendant of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and Totsumachine no Kami, a daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri no Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}.
He is the last deity recorded in the ''Kojiki'' under the lineage of the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, with no further genealogical information provided.
== Shrines Dedicated == * '''Narumi-no-Ki Shrine''' ({{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|WD=Q503411|lt=Inuyama}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}) ** Principal deity. * '''Yamamisaki Shrine''' ({{ill|Unnan|en|Unnan|WD=Q877935|lt=Unnan}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}) ** Located within the grounds of {{ill|Nihara Shrine|ja|日原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as an auxiliary shrine. * '''Kōtari Shrine''' ({{ill|Nagaokakyō, Kyoto|en|Nagaokakyō, Kyoto|WD=Q861246|lt=Nagaokakyō}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}) ** Currently enshrines {{ill|Amatsu Hikone|ja|天神足命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu Hikone|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, though {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|en|Motoori Norinaga|WD=Q384647|lt=Motoori Norinaga}} identified the original deity as Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami.
== Notes == {{Reflist|group="Notes"}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=古事記|date=2014-10-30|publisher=[[Shinchōsha]]|editor=[[Nishimiya Kazutami|西宮一民]]|series=[[Shincho Japanese Classical Compendium]]|isbn=978-4-10-620801-0|ref={{SfnRef|Nishimiya|2014}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} – The grandfather of Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami on his maternal side.
== External Links == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/totsuyamasakitarashinokami/ Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami – Kokugakuin University Classical Culture Project] * [https://inuyama-tabi.com/point/detail/223/ Narumi-no-Ki Shrine | Inuyama Cultural Heritage Navigator] * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/unnan/4af55d34f0d2979455af525d5f9769b71dee03dc.html Nihara Shrine | Shimane Shrine Association] * [https://nagaokatenmangu.or.jp/?page_id=113 Associated Shrines – Nagaoka Tenmangū] (details on Kōtari Shrine)

| Name = Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami | Generation = {{ill|Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|qq=}} 15th generation | Predecessor = {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}} | Successor = None | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | Full Name = 遠津山岬多良斯神 (Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami) | Alternate Name= 遠津山岬帯神 | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = Agricultural deity | Mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|WD=Q60996386|1=Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami}} | mother = {{ill|Totsumachine no Kami|ja|遠津待根神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsumachine no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135330329|1=Totsumatone-no-Kami}} | Relatives = | consort = None recorded | children = None recorded | cult_centre = {{Plainlist| * Narumi-no-Ki Shrine * Yamamisaki Shrine }} | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} }}
'''Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami''' (遠津山岬多良斯神, トオツヤマサキタラシ) is a deity mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}}. He is the last of the Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities. <ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
== Overview == Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami appears exclusively in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'' and is the final deity in the {{ill|Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|qq=}} lineage. He is described as the 14th descendant of the first deity in this lineage, {{ill|Yashimajinumi|en|Yashimajinumi|WD=Q55522907|lt=Yashimajinumi no Kami|1=Yashimajinumi}}. His name is also recorded as '''Totsuyamasakiobi no Kami''' (遠津山岬帯神).
The name "Totsuyamasakitarashi" can be interpreted as: - "Totsu" (遠津): "Far-off" or "distant," inherited from his mother, Totsumachine no Kami. - "Yamasaki" (山岬): Refers to "mountain promontory," often associated with elevated fertile lands enriched by mountain waters. - "Tarashi" (多良斯): Implies "plenitude" or "sufficiency," symbolizing abundance and agricultural prosperity.{{Sfn|Nishimiya|2014|p=384}}
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (from the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]] Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami is the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|qq=}}, himself a descendant of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and Totsumachine no Kami, a daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri no Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}.
He is the last deity recorded in the ''Kojiki'' under the lineage of the {{ill|Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|qq=}}, with no further genealogical information provided.
== Shrines Dedicated == * '''Narumi-no-Ki Shrine''' ({{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|WD=Q503411|lt=Inuyama|1=Inuyama, Aichi}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}) ** Principal deity. * '''Yamamisaki Shrine''' ({{ill|Unnan|en|Unnan|WD=Q877935|lt=Unnan|1=Unnan}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}) ** Located within the grounds of {{ill|Nihara Shrine|ja|日原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as an auxiliary shrine. * '''Kōtari Shrine''' ({{ill|Nagaokakyō, Kyoto|en|Nagaokakyō, Kyoto|WD=Q861246|lt=Nagaokakyō|1=Nagaokakyō, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}) ** Currently enshrines {{ill|Amatsu Hikone|ja|天神足命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu Hikone|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, though {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|en|Motoori Norinaga|WD=Q384647|lt=Motoori Norinaga|1=Motoori Norinaga}} identified the original deity as Totsuyamasakitarashi no Kami.
